findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 34. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: Cover page image/title page layout is not rendered as a distinct cover page in HTML. The PDF has a standalone title page with centered title and author information, while HTML renders it as inline header text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a centered cover page with '2021 White Lake' and 'Aquatic Vegetation Survey' as large centered titles with significant whitespace. HTML renders this as a header block at the top of the content flow.

findings.description: Table 1 and Table 2 in the PDF have color-coded cells (red and green highlighting) in the 2021 column to indicate increases/decreases. The HTML tables preserve this color coding.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show colored cells in the 2021 column - this is consistent.

findings.description: Figure captions and labels appear duplicated in the HTML rendering. Figures are shown twice - once in the flowing web content and once separately, creating duplicate content that disrupts reading order.
findings.evidence: For example, Figure 1 caption 'The 202 predetermined sample points in White Lake' appears in the web flow, then the same figure and caption appear again. Similarly for Figures 2-13, captions and images appear to be duplicated in the HTML.

findings.description: Figure 4 caption text and labels 'A', '2021', 'A' appear as disconnected text fragments between images in the HTML, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Figure 4 as a composite image with panels A, B, C labeled inline. HTML breaks these out with orphaned text labels 'A', '2021', 'A' appearing as separate lines between image elements.

findings.description: Figure 9 has an orphaned 'A' label appearing as standalone text in the HTML between figure renderings.
findings.evidence: PDF Figure 9 is a single map image. HTML shows an 'A' text label appearing between duplicate renderings of the figure.

findings.description: Figure 10 has an orphaned '2020' label appearing as standalone text in the HTML between figure renderings.
findings.evidence: PDF Figure 10 is a single map. HTML shows '2020' text appearing as a separate line near the figure content.

findings.description: Content duplication: stray text 'A' and '2021' from figure labels are repeated as standalone paragraphs in the HTML body, and '2020' appears as a standalone paragraph near Figure 10/11 area, duplicating figure label fragments.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains standalone '

A

' after Figure 4 caption, another '

A

' after Figure 9, and '

2020

' near Figure 11. These are artifacts from PDF page labels ('A', '2021', '2020') that annotated multi-panel figures and should not appear as separate paragraphs.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create broken document structure. There is a <main> inside the outer <main>, which is invalid HTML structure.
findings.evidence: HTML has '<main>' wrapping the entire body, then a '<header>' block, then another '<main>' element inside for the content body.

findings.description: Minor species name discrepancy: the PDF text on page 3 refers to 'Myriophyllum tentellum' (likely a typo in the original), while the HTML reproduces this faithfully. However, in the same paragraph the PDF also says 'slender spikerush' while the report body later uses 'Proliferating spikerush' - this exists in the source PDF and is not a conversion error.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML: 'dwarf milfoil (Myriophyllum tentellum)' in one place and 'Myriophyllum tenellum' in another. This inconsistency exists in the source PDF.

findings.description: One image (tables page) has an empty alt text attribute, providing no description.
findings.evidence: HTML: '' for the tables image on page 8.

findings.description: A small extraneous image tag appears near Figure 10 with empty alt text, likely an artifact.
findings.evidence: HTML: '' appears between Figure 10 and its caption.
